messages.content:
Complete the sentences with the correct form of the word in CAPITALS.

 I think she’s now got a very [answer] job in the government. INFLUENCE
I think she’s now got a very influential job in the government.

Complete the sentences with the correct form of the word in CAPITALS.

They were sent home from school for [answer] their teacher. OBEY
They were sent home from school for disobeying their teacher.

Complete the sentences with the correct form of the word in CAPITALS.

If you’d arrived on time, Dad wouldn’t have been so [answer]. ANNOY
If you’d arrived on time, Dad wouldn’t have been so annoyed.

Choose the reponse that is NOT possible.
Is it OK to offer to split the bill when you are invited to eat out in Thailand?


Sorry, I didn’t know.
I’m fairly sure it’s considered rude to do that.
No, you’d better not, as the host expects to pay.
Sorry, I didn’t know.

Choose the reponse that is NOT possible.
What should we get Duncan and Jo for their wedding anniversary?


I don't know.
You really don't have to.
If I were you, I’d just send them a card.
I don't know.

Complete the conversation.

A: Have  blank my car keys, Anna?
B: I’m fairly  blank I left them on the table.
A: No, I’ve looked there. Did you put them in your handbag?
B: It’s possible. I can’t  blank . Where is my handbag anyway?
A: I  blank  a clue. You know, I think we should buy a red carpet!
B: Really? Why red?
A: I read an article about it. Apparently red helps you remember details better.
B: What exactly  blank mean?
A: Well, for one  blank  , we’re getting very forgetful these days.
B: Yes, but the way I  blank , that’s because we spend lots of time using computers and rely on them too much.
A:  blank ! I think I’ll try using a red screen on mine and see if it makes a difference.


blank: sure haven't got thing Exactly do you see it remember you seen

A: Have you seen my car keys, Anna?
B: I’m fairly sure I left them on the table.
A: No, I’ve looked there. Did you put them in your handbag?
B: It’s possible. I can’t be sure. Where is my handbag anyway?
A: I haven't got a clue. You know, I think we should buy a red carpet!
B: Really? Why red?
A: I read an article about it. Apparently red helps you remember details better.
B: What exactly do you see it mean?
A: Well, for one thing, we’re getting very forgetful these days.
B: Yes, but the way I see it, that’s because we spend lots of time using computers and rely on them too much.
A: Exactly! I think I’ll try using a red screen on mine and see if it makes a difference.

Complete the sentence.

 blank  a thing. I’ll find someone else who can help with the project.

blank: I’m afraid I can’t You’re joking    Whatever you You lucky thing    You’ll never guess I’m sorry but     Don’t worry about
I’m sorry but I can’t. I’ll find someone else who can help with the project.

Complete the sentence.

 blank ! They can’t be getting divorced already – they only got married two weeks ago!

blank: You lucky thing    I’m sorry but    I’m afraid I can’t Whatever you  Don’t worry about You’re joking    You’ll never guess
You're joking! They can’t be getting divorced already – they only got married two weeks ago!

Give the word to the definition.

the process of adjusting the space between characters
Kerning.

Give the word to the definition.

the drawing of a model by using features like edges or contour lines
Outline.

Translate into English.

таблицы (как вид программы)
spreadsheets (as a software type)

Complete the sentence with one word.

A ___ protects a company intranet from outside attacks.
Firewall.

Give the word to the definition.

the number of pixels in an image
Resolution.

Translate into English.

запрос
Query.

Complete the sentence with a job name.

A ___ writes computer programs.
Programmer.

Give the word to the definition.

a computer programming technique that allows the creation of objects that interact with each other and can be used as the foundation of others; used to create graphical user interfaces
Object-oriented programming (OOP).

Give the word to the definition.

a machine that creates the printing plates
Platemaker.

Translate into English.

цифровая видеокамера
Digital video camera.

What type of cybercrime is it?

getting passwords for online bank accounts or credit card numbers by using emails that look like they are from real organizations, but are in fact fake; people believe the message is from their bank and send their security details
Phishing.

Give the word to the definition.

The techniques of detecting and correcting errors (or bugs) which may occur in programs
Debugging.

Give the word to the definition.

a technique for playing sound and video files while they're downloading
Streaming.

Give the word to the definition.

the process of manipulating video images
Video editing.

Translate into English

горизонтальная линейка
Horizontal ruler.

Complete the sentence with a job name.

A ___ works with companies to build secure computer systems.
Security consultant.

Give the word to the definition.

the underlying colours and patterns of a web page
Background.

Choose the correct variants.

A delicious meal

My most delicious meal ever was chicken soup. ___, it wasn’t just the soup that was special ___ also where and when it was eaten. I was working in Oman ___Ramadan, when Muslims don’t eat or drink from sunrise to sunset. ___ I’m not a Muslim, I admired the willpower of my friends so much ___ I joined them. It was ___ one of the most memorable days of my life ___ it was full of emotion. ___ the sun had begun to set, crowds of people gathered in the square ___ the cafés had prepared bowls of steaming chicken soup ___ sweet desserts. ___ the sun went down, we all tasted our first mouthful of soup. Delicious!

My most delicious meal ever was chicken soup. Not only was it delicious, but it was also where and when it was eaten. I was working in Oman during Ramadan, when Muslims don’t eat or drink from sunrise to sunset. Although I’m not a Muslim, I admired the willpower of my friends so much that I joined them. It was definitely one of the most memorable days of my life as it was full of emotion. As the sun had begun to set, crowds of people gathered in the square where the cafés had prepared bowls of steaming chicken soup and sweet desserts. As the sun went down, we all tasted our first mouthful of soup. Delicious!

Choose the correct variants.

A delicious meal

My most delicious meal ever was chicken soup. (However / Furthermore), it wasn’t just the soup that was special (because / but) also where and when it was eaten. I was working in Oman (while / during) Ramadan, when Muslims don’t eat or drink from sunrise to sunset. (Although / However) I’m not a Muslim, I admired the willpower of my friends so much (than / that) I joined them. It was (probably / might) one of the most memorable days of my life (in order to / because) it was full of emotion. (Finaly / By the time) the sun had begun to set, crowds of people gathered in the square (which / where) the cafés had prepared bowls of steaming chicken soup (also / as well as) sweet desserts. (Until / As soon as) the sun went down, we all tasted our first mouthful of soup. Delicious!

My most delicious meal ever was chicken soup. Furthermore, it wasn’t just the soup that was special but also where and when it was eaten. I was working in Oman during Ramadan, when Muslims don’t eat or drink from sunrise to sunset. Although I’m not a Muslim, I admired the willpower of my friends so much that I joined them. It might have been one of the most memorable days of my life because it was full of emotion. By the time the sun had begun to set, crowds of people gathered in the square where the cafés had prepared bowls of steaming chicken soup as well as sweet desserts. As soon as the sun went down, we all tasted our first mouthful of soup. Delicious!

Read the article and choose the correct heading for each paragraph.

 (If at first you don’t succeed, try and try again / Americans conquer the Captain / Getting past the worst of it / Not a hobby for the faint-hearted / Perseverance pays off / Feet off the earth)
Free climbing is something that takes a lot of strength and courage. It involves climbing rocks using just your hands and feet. In other words, you use ropes and equipment but these do not help you climb. Instead, they provide support if you fall.

(If at first you don’t succeed, try and try again / Americans conquer the Captain / Getting past the worst of it / Not a hobby for the faint-hearted / Perseverance pays off / Feet off the earth)
In 2014, Americans Tommy Caldwell and Kevin Jorgeson became the first people to climb an extremely difficult part of the rock El Capitan.
The section they climbed is called Yosemite’s Dawn Wall, and is about 3,000 feet high. In climbing terms, it is divided into thirty-two rope-length sections, which are known as ‘pitches’.

(If at first you don’t succeed, try and try again / Americans conquer the Captain / Getting past the worst of it / Not a hobby for the faint-hearted / Perseverance pays off / Feet off the earth)
Caldwell and Jorgeson’s aim was to become the first climbers to free climb the complete set of thirty-two pitches. But they were also going to do it without stopping to return to the ground. What’s more, they decided that if either of them did fall during a pitch, they would restart and complete that pitch before they could continue.

(If at first you don’t succeed, try and try again / Americans conquer the Captain / Getting past the worst of it / Not a hobby for the faint-hearted / Perseverance pays off / Feet off the earth)
The first 14 pitches, which were said to include some of the more difficult parts of the climb, took them only six days to complete. This in itself was a personal record for them as they had never before got further than pitch 13 in the previous years of attempting to climb El Capitan.

(If at first you don’t succeed, try and try again / Americans conquer the Captain / Getting past the worst of it / Not a hobby for the faint-hearted / Perseverance pays off / Feet off the earth)
Two weeks into the climb, Caldwell had managed to free climb an impressive twenty out of the total thirty-two pitches while Jorgeson was having a lot of trouble with pitch 15. For ten days he had repeatedly tried to complete this section but without success. He knew that if he couldn’t complete it, Caldwell would have to continue alone. This was not something either one of the climbers wanted.

(If at first you don’t succeed, try and try again / Americans conquer the Captain / Getting past the worst of it / Not a hobby for the faint-hearted / Perseverance pays off / Feet off the earth)
Fortunately, on his eleventh attempt, Jorgeson managed to finish pitch 15 and in a matter of days, both climbers had reached a height of 2,000 feet with only the easier climbing left above them. After an incredible nineteen days spent on the rock, the two young men succeeded in reaching the *peak of El Capitan. They had achieved their record!

1. Not a hobby for the faint-hearted
2. Americans conquer the Captain
3. Perseverance pays off
4. Getting past the worst of it
5. If at first you don’t succeed, try and try again
6. Feet off the earth

He said it’d be quicker to go by car but I’d probably have

(to try something new, take it or you’ll regret it for the rest of your life! / a seat for the match. / dramas, don’t you? / trouble finding a parking space. / should meet her in the main square? / strategy when studying a language with a different alphabet to your own? / results of this test?
